title: Replace `when not is_nil(req)` with an assertive `when is_binary(req)`
prompt: Refactor MyApp.Validator.call_service/1 from test_fixtures/bad_guards.ex. The current guard `when not is_nil(req)` is the Credo.NegatedIsNil anti-pattern — replace it with an assertive guard `when is_binary(req)`. Also refactor the `process/1` function to use an assertive guard `when is_map(thing)` (or `when is_binary(thing)`) instead of `when not is_nil(thing)`. Keep the fallback clauses. Output the complete refactored module as `defmodule MyApp.Validator do ... end` with both functions (you may drop `validate_age/1` / `validate_email/1` / `format_user/1`).
tier_rationale: Direct Credo rule application. The fix is a known anti-pattern and there is only one correct direction.
research_citation: Credo.Check.Refactor.NegatedIsNil and Elixir Forum idiom guidance: 'don't use def call_service(%{req: req}) when not is_nil(req), instead use when is_binary(req)'
